name: TeamSpeak 3
author: ops@refx.example
description: TeamSpeak 3 voice server — low-latency VoIP for gaming communities. Slot-based: priced per simultaneous user.
longDescription: Host a TeamSpeak 3 voice server with crystal-clear, low-latency audio. Voice servers are billed per slot (simultaneous users) and run on lightweight resources compared to game servers. Connect on the assigned voice port shown in your panel. The purchased slot count is passed to the container as TS3SERVER_MAX_CLIENTS / SLOTS; the ServerQuery admin token (and privilege key) is printed to the console on first boot — TeamSpeak enforces the per-virtual-server slot cap via ServerQuery.

installScript.script:
#!/bin/ash
set -e
# TeamSpeak ships in its Docker image; we only drop in the ReFx launcher.
mkdir -p /mnt/server
cat > /mnt/server/refx-ts3-run.sh <<'RUNEOF'
#!/bin/sh
# ReFx launcher for TeamSpeak 3. POSIX sh + busybox nc — the official teamspeak
# image is Alpine (no bash, no /dev/tcp, no process substitution). Runs
# ts3server, captures the ServerQuery admin token, enforces the slot cap, and
# snapshots live status + bandwidth for the panel's Voice tab.
set -u
cd /home/container 2>/dev/null || cd "$(dirname "$0")"

SLOTS="${SLOTS:-${TS3SERVER_MAX_CLIENTS:-32}}"
VOICE_PORT="${SERVER_PORT:-9987}"
QUERY_PORT=10011
BOOTLOG="$PWD/.refx-ts3-boot.log"
INFO="$PWD/refx-voice.json"
STATUS="$PWD/refx-voice-status.txt"
CMD="$PWD/refx-voice-cmd.txt"
BW="$PWD/refx-voice-bw.csv"
: > "$BOOTLOG"

SRV=
shutdown() {
  # Graceful stop: signal ts3server directly (SRV is the tee pipeline).
  _p="$(pidof ts3server 2>/dev/null)"
  [ -n "$_p" ] && kill -TERM $_p 2>/dev/null
}
trap shutdown TERM INT

# Minimal TS3 ServerQuery over busybox nc (localhost telnet protocol).
ts3query() {
  qpw="$1"; shift
  {
    printf 'login serveradmin %s\n' "$qpw"
    printf 'use sid=1\n'
    for c in "$@"; do printf '%s\n' "$c"; done
    printf 'quit\n'
  } | nc -w 5 127.0.0.1 "$QUERY_PORT" 2>/dev/null
}

port_up() { printf '' | nc -w 2 127.0.0.1 "$QUERY_PORT" >/dev/null 2>&1; }

manage() {
  up=""; i=0
  while [ "$i" -lt 120 ]; do
    if port_up; then up=1; break; fi
    i=$((i + 1)); sleep 1
  done
  [ -n "$up" ] || { echo "[refx] ServerQuery never came up; skipping slot enforcement."; return; }

  pw=""; token=""; i=0
  while [ "$i" -lt 20 ]; do
    pw="$(grep -m1 'password=' "$BOOTLOG" 2>/dev/null | sed -n 's/.*password=[[:space:]]*"\([^"]*\)".*/\1/p')"
    { [ -n "$pw" ] || [ -f "$INFO" ]; } && break
    i=$((i + 1)); sleep 1
  done
  token="$(grep -m1 'token=' "$BOOTLOG" 2>/dev/null | sed -n 's/.*token=\([A-Za-z0-9+/=]*\).*/\1/p')"

  if [ -z "$pw" ] && [ -f "$INFO" ]; then
    pw="$(sed -n 's/.*"queryPassword":"\([^"]*\)".*/\1/p' "$INFO")"
    [ -z "$token" ] && token="$(sed -n 's/.*"privilegeKey":"\([^"]*\)".*/\1/p' "$INFO")"
  fi
  [ -n "$pw" ] || { echo "[refx] Could not read TeamSpeak admin credentials yet."; return; }

  printf '{"queryAdmin":"serveradmin","queryPassword":"%s","queryPort":%s,"privilegeKey":"%s","slots":%s}\n' \
    "$pw" "$QUERY_PORT" "$token" "$SLOTS" > "$INFO"
  chmod 600 "$INFO" 2>/dev/null || true

  if ts3query "$pw" "serveredit virtualserver_maxclients=$SLOTS" | grep -q 'error id=0'; then
    echo "[refx] Enforced TeamSpeak slot limit: $SLOTS clients."
  else
    echo "[refx] Could not apply slot limit automatically (will retry next restart)."
  fi
  echo "[refx] TeamSpeak admin details saved — see the Voice tab in the panel."
}

monitor() {
  pw=""; i=0; j=0
  while [ "$j" -lt 180 ]; do
    [ -f "$INFO" ] && pw="$(sed -n 's/.*"queryPassword":"\([^"]*\)".*/\1/p' "$INFO")"
    [ -n "$pw" ] && break
    j=$((j + 1)); sleep 2
  done
  [ -n "$pw" ] || return
  while kill -0 "$SRV" 2>/dev/null; do
    if [ -s "$CMD" ]; then
      cmds="$(cat "$CMD" 2>/dev/null)"; rm -f "$CMD" 2>/dev/null
      printf '%s\n' "$cmds" | while IFS= read -r c; do
        [ -n "$c" ] && ts3query "$pw" "$c" >/dev/null 2>&1
      done
    fi
    if [ $((i % 3)) -eq 0 ]; then
      { ts3query "$pw" "serverinfo" "clientlist" "channellist -limits" "banlist"; echo "refx_ts=$(date +%s)"; } > "$STATUS.tmp" 2>/dev/null \
        && mv "$STATUS.tmp" "$STATUS" 2>/dev/null
      down=$(grep -o 'connection_bandwidth_received_last_second_total=[0-9]*' "$STATUS" 2>/dev/null | head -1 | cut -d= -f2)
      up=$(grep -o 'connection_bandwidth_sent_last_second_total=[0-9]*' "$STATUS" 2>/dev/null | head -1 | cut -d= -f2)
      echo "$(date +%s),${down:-0},${up:-0}" >> "$BW"
      tail -n 120 "$BW" > "$BW.tmp" 2>/dev/null && mv "$BW.tmp" "$BW" 2>/dev/null
    fi
    i=$((i + 1)); sleep 5
  done
}

# ts3server writes to the boot log; tail -f mirrors it to the panel console
# (busybox has no process substitution, so we can't pipe + keep the PID).
# Accepted above — make both the env and the runscript arg agree.
export TS3SERVER_LICENSE=accept
export LD_LIBRARY_PATH="/opt/ts3server:${LD_LIBRARY_PATH:-}"
mkdir -p logs files 2>/dev/null || true
# Run ts3server from the WRITABLE data dir (CWD is /home/container, set above) so
# its logs/, database (ts3server.sqlitedb) and files/ land on our volume. The
# image's own runscript cd's into /opt/ts3server, which the non-root game user
# can't write -> "createDirectory logs 13" / "unable to open database file".
# dbsqlpath points at the SQL scripts that live (read-only) in the image. tee
# mirrors output to the panel console AND the boot log for credential capture.
/opt/ts3server/ts3server \
  default_voice_port="$VOICE_PORT" \
  query_port="$QUERY_PORT" \
  dbsqlpath=/opt/ts3server/sql/ \
  license_accepted=1 \
  2>&1 | tee "$BOOTLOG" &
SRV=$!

manage &
monitor &

while kill -0 "$SRV" 2>/dev/null; do wait "$SRV" 2>/dev/null; done
RUNEOF
chmod +x /mnt/server/refx-ts3-run.sh
echo 'TeamSpeak 3 ready — accept the license and connect on the assigned voice port.'

variables.description: Required by the TeamSpeak image to run. Customer acceptance is enforced separately on the Voice tab.
Maximum simultaneous users. Set automatically from the slot count you purchase.
